creator: eblake@redhat.com
text:
divert( -1 ) is the same as divert(`-1 ').

POSIX says:

It shall be an error to specify an argument containing any non-numeric characters.

The leading space is eaten (divert never sees it), but the trailing space is non-numeric, so the code is correct that you have supplied invalid input, and it can therefore do whatever it wants.  However, whether "whatever it wants" means ignoring the trailing space, printing an error, or anything else is a matter of quality of implementation; and if that behavior has changed over time you can certainly complain about regressions.  And in this case, `m4 -g' exiting on invalid input is not the same as GNU m4's behavior of printing the warning but then proceeding without changing the current diversion number.
